description: Skills for academic paragraph polishing, anti-AI text patterns, Chinese AI-flavor removal, and SCI-style scientific writing. Targeted at Chinese SCI Q1 authors as primary user persona.
selection_criteria: Compatible with Anthropic SKILL.md format (or trivially adaptable)
License compatible with MIT for packaged recommendations, or explicitly marked license-review-needed for remote comparison only
At least one of: 100+ stars, clear academic identity, Chinese first-class support, or rapidly emerging anti-AI writing skill
Diversity: covers anti-AI + academic-style across zh + en (2x2 matrix, each cell ≥ 1)

skills.name: humanizer
skills.author: blader
skills.approach: Names 20+ specific AI writing patterns, does two-pass detection
skills.notes: Most rigorous anti-AI approach in the catalog. Strong on English text.

skills.name: writing-anti-ai
skills.author: Galaxy-Dawn
skills.approach: Bilingual anti-AI patterns: inflated symbolism, em dash overuse, rule of three, AI vocab, etc.
skills.notes: Only catalog entry with first-class Chinese support. Critical for primary user persona. Lives in a multi-skill repo; subdir required.

skills.name: Humanizer-zh
skills.author: op7418
skills.approach: Chinese adaptation of humanizer-style rules for removing signs of AI-generated writing.
skills.notes: High-star Chinese Claude Code Skills project focused on eliminating AI-generated traces in Chinese text.

skills.name: stop-slop
skills.author: hardikpandya
skills.approach: Rule file for removing AI tells from prose and reducing generic slop.
skills.notes: Very popular SKILL.md-style candidate for English prose with strong anti-slop positioning.

skills.name: avoid-ai-writing
skills.author: conorbronsdon
skills.approach: Audits and rewrites content to remove AI writing patterns across agent surfaces.
skills.notes: Clear SKILL.md repo for audit plus rewrite workflows; useful as an English anti-AI comparison path.

skills.name: remove-ai-flavor-writing-skill
skills.author: B1lli
skills.approach: Compact Chinese AI-flavor removal for template sentence shells, fake engagement endings, and over-polished copy.
skills.notes: Codex-oriented Chinese 去 AI 味 skill; fits social copy, public writing, and naturalization tasks.

skills.name: academic-humanizer
skills.author: AIScientists-Dev
skills.approach: Removes AI-writing tells from papers and grant proposals while preserving scholarly voice and evidence ties.
skills.notes: Academic-specific humanizer for papers and grants. License needs review before redistribution, but remote BYO comparison is supported.

skills.name: humanizer_academic
skills.author: matsuikentaro1
skills.approach: Removes signs of AI-generated writing from academic medical papers.
skills.notes: Medical academic writing candidate; useful for biomedical manuscript polishing comparisons.

skills.name: patina
skills.author: devswha
skills.approach: Multilingual AI-writing humanizer for Korean, English, Chinese, and Japanese.
skills.notes: Useful when the task explicitly asks for multilingual anti-AI rewriting beyond Chinese and English.

skills.name: HumanAI
skills.author: MADEVAL
skills.approach: Five-stage pipeline for rewriting machine-generated text to sound human-written across multiple languages.
skills.notes: Lower-star but structured multilingual humanization skill; keep as an extended candidate rather than default.

skills.name: Research-Paper-Writing-Skills
skills.author: Master-cai (curated from Peng Sida's open notes)
skills.approach: ML/CV/NLP paper writing conventions, from Chinese academic perspective
skills.notes: Authored by Chinese academic — high resonance with target user community (中文 SCI 投稿者). Skill lives in research-paper-writing subdir.

skills.name: paper-writer-skill
skills.author: kgraph57
skills.approach: Full IMRAD pipeline, medical/scientific focus, manuscript lifecycle including reviewer response
skills.notes: Complement to research-paper-writing-skills — covers medical/biology side of SCI authorship. WARNING: This is a multi-step workflow skill (58KB SKILL.md) designed for tool use; in v0.1's single-turn harness it often hits max_turns. Better suited for v0.2 when we support multi-turn execution.
